title: Spain vs. Saudi Arabia: Team news, lineups & key stats | World Cup 2026 - IRNA English
original_text:
Spain vs. Saudi Arabia: Team news, lineups & key stats | World Cup 2026
Spain faces Saudi Arabia in a crucial World Cup clash. Get team news, injuries, expected lineups, and how to watch La Roja’s Group H battle live.
Spain will be desperate to secure their first victory of the 2026 World Cup when they face Saudi Arabia in a pivotal Group H encounter on Sunday afternoon at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta. La Roja were held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by Cape Verde in their tournament opener, while the Green Falcons showed their resilience by earning a 1-1 draw against Uruguay. With all four teams in the group level on one point, this match carries enormous significance for both sides’ qualification hopes, and Spain know that anything less than three points would increase the pressure heading into their final group game against Uruguay.
Team News & Injuries
Spain Injury Updates
Luis de la Fuente faces a delicate balancing act with his team selection. Lamine Yamal remains a fitness concern despite making a positive impact as a substitute against Cape Verde, playing the final 19 minutes after recovering from a hamstring injury sustained while representing Barcelona at the end of April. The 18-year-old superstar is expected to be introduced into the starting lineup, with Spain confident he is now ready to feature from the first whistle, though questions remain over whether he can complete a full 90 minutes.
New Liverpool signing Víctor Muñoz may again be unavailable for selection due to a hamstring issue that has kept him out of contention. Mikel Merino is also nursing a knock, though the extent of his injury has not been fully disclosed. Fellow forward Nico Williams could be introduced into the starting XI following his late appearance against Cape Verde, with Ferran Torres and Gavi likely to drop to the bench as De la Fuente looks for more attacking impetus.
Saudi Arabia Injury Updates
Saudi Arabia has not reported any new injury problems following their opening match against Uruguay. They are expected to have a fully fit squad available for selection.
Expected Lineups
Spain Possible XI (4-3-3): Simon; Llorente, Cubarsi, Laporte, Cucurella; Ruiz, Rodri, Pedri; Yamal, Oyarzabal, N Williams
Saudi Arabia Possible XI (4-4-2): Al-Owais; Abdulhamid, Al Tambakti, Al Amri, Al Harbi; Al Shamat, Al-Khaibari, Kanno, S Al-Dawsari; Al Buraikan, Al-Juwayr
Timeline & Recent Form
Last 5 Matches
Spain:
Spain 0-0 Cape Verde
Spain 3-1 Peru
Spain 1-1 Iraq
Spain 0-0 Egypt
Spain 3-0 Serbia
Saudi Arabia:
Saudi Arabia 1-1 Uruguay
Senegal 0-0 Saudi Arabia
Saudi Arabia 3-0 Puerto Rico
Ecuador 2-1 Saudi Arabia
Serbia 2-1 Saudi Arabia
Head-to-Head Record & Key Stats
This will be the second time the two nations meet at a World Cup finals. Spain recorded a 1-0 victory in their group-stage fixture at the 2006 tournament. La Roja has also won two friendly matches against the Green Falcons.
Key Stats
All four teams in Group H are currently level on one point after the first round of matches.
Saudi Arabia is seeking its first-ever victory against Spain.
In their opener, Spain generated 2.29 Expected Goals (xG) and had 27 shots but failed to score against Cape Verde.
Abdulelah Al Amri scored for Saudi Arabia in their 1-1 draw with Uruguay, while Spain was held scoreless by Cape Verde.
Match Information & Broadcast
Spain and Saudi Arabia meet for just the second time at a World Cup finals, with La Roja having won their only previous encounter 1-0 at the 2006 tournament. The match takes place on Sunday, June 21, at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta, marking another chapter in what has become a fascinating Group H battle.
How to Watch Spain vs Saudi Arabia on TV, Live Stream
Date: Sunday, June 21
Kick-off Times:
12:00 p.m. ET (USA)
5:00 p.m. BST (UK)
9:30 p.m. IST (India)
2:00 a.m. AEST (Monday, June 22 in Australia)
Stadium: Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta, USA
TV Channels & Streaming:
UK: BBC One
U.S.: Fox Sports
India: Zee5
Australia: SBS
Venue: Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta, USA
Referee: Raphael Claus (Brazil)
Match Analysis Summary
This match represents a must-win situation for Spain if they are to avoid mounting pressure in their final group game against Uruguay. De la Fuente faces a critical decision over Lamine Yamal’s involvement, with the teenager’s cameo against Cape Verde demonstrating his unique ability to unlock stubborn defenses. Even if Yamal cannot complete a full 90 minutes, starting with him would send a positive message and potentially allow Spain to build an early lead before managing his minutes.
The longer the game remains goalless, the more dangerous it becomes for Spain. They created ample chances against Cape Verde but lacked the finishing touch, and clinical conversion will be paramount against a Saudi Arabia side that will look to replicate Cape Verde’s defensive blueprint while offering greater threat on the counter-attack. For the Green Falcons, transitions through Al-Dawsari and set-pieces represent their best routes to goal. An organized defensive display combined with clinical finishing at key moments could produce another shock result, though Spain’s quality should eventually tell if they can find their rhythm early and avoid the anxiety that crept into their Cape Verde performance.
